title: Thawing, Expanding, and Freezing Human Fibroblasts
author: Jacob Marsh
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bgunjwve

steps.title: Prior to thawing cells, prepare culture media
steps.instruction: Prior to thawing cells, prepare culture media

steps.title: hFibroblast Media
steps.instruction: hFibroblast Media: DMEM/F12, 10-20% FBS 1% L-Glut (or Glutamax) 1% Antibiotic

steps.title: Remove cells from liquid nitrogen storage...
steps.instruction: Remove cells from liquid nitrogen storage and thaw quickly by placing vial in a 37 °C water bath for approximately 00:01:00 Be sure to monitor cells during thaw and make sure they are removed from water bath just prior to completely thawing

steps.title: Quickly transfer thawed cells into conical...
steps.instruction: Quickly transfer thawed cells into conical tube containing 9 mL of room temperature DMEM/F12

steps.title: 750 rpm, Room temperature, 3 minutes
steps.instruction: 750 rpm, Room temperature, 3 minutes

steps.title: Aspirate supernatant, be careful not to distrub cell pellet
steps.instruction: Aspirate supernatant, be careful not to distrub cell pellet

steps.title: Re-suspend cells in 5 mL of...
steps.instruction: Re-suspend cells in 5 mL of hFibroblast Media by pipetting cells up and down 2-3 times to achieve a single cell suspension

steps.title: Transfer cell suspension to T25 cell culture flask
steps.instruction: Transfer cell suspension to T25 cell culture flask

steps.title: Incubate at 37 °C and 5% C02 overnight
steps.instruction: Incubate at 37 °C and 5% C02 overnight

steps.title: Replace hFibroblast Media every 2-3 days
steps.instruction: Replace hFibroblast Media every 2-3 days

steps.title: Upon reaching 80-90% confluency, fibroblasts will create a swirling pattern
steps.instruction: Upon reaching 80-90% confluency, fibroblasts will create a swirling pattern. This is the appropriate time to split the cells Confluent fibroblasts can be split from one T25 cell culture flask into one T75 cell culture flask OR two T25 cell culture flasks.

steps.title: Aspirate hFibroblast Media from T25 cell culture flask
steps.instruction: Aspirate hFibroblast Media from T25 cell culture flask

steps.title: Wash cells with 5 mL 1X PBS
steps.instruction: Wash cells with 5 mL 1X PBS

steps.title: Add 1 mL of 0
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL of 0.25% Trypsin to cells. Incubate at 37 °C for 00:05:00 to allow cells to dissociate from cell culture flask After incubation period, check cells under a microscope to determine the extent to which cells have detached from the flask. If many cells remain attached, incubate at 37 °C for an additional 00:05:00 .

steps.title: Re-suspend cells in 5 mL of hFibroblast Media
steps.instruction: Re-suspend cells in 5 mL of hFibroblast Media. Pipet cells up and wash flask 4-6 times to collect all cells and to achieve a single cell suspension.

steps.title: Add cell suspension to 7 mL...
steps.instruction: Add cell suspension to 7 mL of fresh hFibroblast Media and transfer to T75 cell culture flask

steps.title: Incubate at 37 °C at 5% C02 overnight
steps.instruction: Incubate at 37 °C at 5% C02 overnight

steps.title: Change media every 2-3 days until...
steps.instruction: Change media every 2-3 days until fibroblasts become confluent At this point fibroblasts can be passaged again, used for experiments, or frozen down and banked

steps.title: Upon reaching 80-90% confluency, fibroblasts will create a swirling pattern
steps.instruction: Upon reaching 80-90% confluency, fibroblasts will create a swirling pattern. This is the appropriate time to split the cells It is important to keep fibroblast cultures at a low passage number, thus when thawing cells it should be a priority to expand cells for freezing over expanding cells for experiments. One T75 cell culture flask will produce enough cells to freeze 2-3 1 mL aliquots.

steps.title: Aspirate hFibroblast Media from T25 cell culture flask
steps.instruction: Aspirate hFibroblast Media from T25 cell culture flask

steps.title: Wash cells with 5 mL 1X PBS
steps.instruction: Wash cells with 5 mL 1X PBS

steps.title: Add 2 mL of 0
steps.instruction: Add 2 mL of 0.25% Trypsin to cells. Incubate at 37 °C for 00:05:00 to allow cells to dissociate from cell culture flask

steps.title: Re-suspend cells in 7 mL of hFibroblast Media
steps.instruction: Re-suspend cells in 7 mL of hFibroblast Media. Pipet cells up and wash flask 4-6 times to collect all cells and to achieve a single cell suspension.

steps.title: Transfer cell suspension to conical tube for centrifugation
steps.instruction: Transfer cell suspension to conical tube for centrifugation

steps.title: 500 rpm, Room temperature, 5 minutes
steps.instruction: 500 rpm, Room temperature, 5 minutes

steps.title: Aspirate supernatant, be careful not to distrub cell pellet
steps.instruction: Aspirate supernatant, be careful not to distrub cell pellet

steps.title: Re-suspend cell pellet in 1
steps.instruction: Re-suspend cell pellet in 1.5 mL hFibroblast Media.

steps.title: Add 1
steps.instruction: Add 1.5 mL 2X Freezing Media (FBS, 10% DMSO) to cell suspenion from Step 26. This should result in a final volume of 3 mL which can be equally distributed into 1 mL aliquots across three cryovials.

steps.title: Transfer 1 mL to each pre-labeled cryovial
steps.instruction: Transfer 1 mL to each pre-labeled cryovial

steps.title: Immediately place cells in -80 °C freezer for 72:00:00
steps.instruction: Immediately place cells in -80 °C freezer for 72:00:00

steps.title: Transfer cells to long-term liquid nitrogen storage
steps.instruction: Transfer cells to long-term liquid nitrogen storage
